column1: Xavier Edwards leads the slate with a strong combination of recent form and reliability, collecting hits in nearly 75% of games and benefiting from a favorable platoon edge against Michael Lorenzen in a hitter-friendly park. Otto Lopez offers similar appeal with steady season-long production near the top of the order and an even higher rate of games with a hit. Jake McCarthy and Liam Hicks also draw soft matchups with platoon advantages, but Edwards and Lopez stand out for consistency and opportunity.
